materials.name: deciduous_leaf_green
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Aspen green-top leaf (ASD 350-2500nm)

materials.name: deciduous_leaf_autumn
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Aspen yellow/autumn top leaf (ASD)

materials.name: grass_green
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Juncus roemerianus green rush (ASD)
materials.notes: Green rush/grass measured in Delacroix Marsh, LA

materials.name: grass_dry
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Golden dry grass GDS480 (ASD)

materials.name: conifer_needle
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Lodgepole pine needles (ASD)

materials.name: tree_bark_dark
materials.source: ECOSTRESS: Pinus ponderosa bark VSWIR ASD (UCSB HyspIRI)

materials.name: tree_bark_light
materials.source: ECOSTRESS: Calocedrus decurrens (incense cedar) bark VSWIR ASD (UCSB)

materials.name: lichen_moss
materials.source: ECOSTRESS: Lichen species VSWIR ASD (UCSB HyspIRI)

materials.name: soil_dark
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Stonewall Playa dry mud (ASD)

materials.name: soil_sandy
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Illite+Quartz (ASD, bright sandy clay mineral)
materials.notes: Proxy for light sandy soil; actual mineral composition typical of sandy soils

materials.name: sand_beach
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Grand Isle beach sand, no oil (ASD)

materials.name: gravel_basalt
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Pyroxene basalt CU01-20A (ASD)

materials.name: snow
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Melting snow mSnw01a (ASD)

materials.name: water_clear
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Water + 0.5 g/L montmorillonite (ASD; essentially clear water)
materials.notes: Very dilute clay suspension; spectrally equivalent to clear water in 300-2500nm range

materials.name: concrete_grey
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Concrete GDS375 light grey road (ASD)

materials.name: asphalt_dark
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Asphalt GDS376 black road (old, ASD)

materials.name: brick_red
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Brick GDS349 paving red (ASD)

materials.name: ceramic_tile_white
materials.source: USGS splib07a: TiO2 pigment GDS798 (ASD) — white ceramic tile proxy
materials.notes: TiO2 is the primary pigment in white ceramic tiles and white paint

materials.name: clear_glass
materials.source: Computed: Fresnel R from soda-lime glass n(λ) dispersion (Rubin 1985)
materials.notes: n≈1.52 from Rubin 1985 Solar Energy Materials 12:275-288; single-surface reflectance

materials.name: plaster_stucco
materials.source: USGS splib07a: WTC01-37A concrete/plaster (ASD)

materials.name: roof_tile_terracotta
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Brick GDS350 dark red building brick (ASD)

materials.name: limestone_marble
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Limestone CU02-11A (ASD)

materials.name: aluminum_polished
materials.source: Computed: Rakić et al. 1998 Lorentz-Drude model for Al, Appl. Opt. 37(22):5271

materials.name: steel_brushed
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Galvanized sheet metal GDS334 (ASD)

materials.name: copper_oxidized
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Synthetic malachite GDS801 (ASD) — oxidised copper patina
materials.notes: CuCO3·Cu(OH)2; the green patina on oxidised copper surfaces

materials.name: gold_polished
materials.source: Computed: Rakić et al. 1998 Lorentz-Drude model for Au, Appl. Opt. 37(22):5271

materials.name: rust_iron_oxide
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Rusted tin can GDS378 (ASD)

materials.name: cotton_white
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Cotton fabric GDS437 white (ASD)

materials.name: cotton_dark
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Nylon fabric GDS436 black coated (ASD)
materials.notes: Black synthetic fabric; representative of dark/dyed cotton

materials.name: wool_burlap
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Burlap fabric GDS430 brown (ASD)
materials.notes: Coarse natural fiber fabric; representative of wool/jute

materials.name: wood_pine
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Wood beam GDS363 new pine 2x4 (ASD)
materials.notes: Fresh pine; representative of light natural wood

materials.name: paper_white
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Paper cotton bond PAPR1 100% (ASD)

materials.name: rubber_black
materials.source: ECOSTRESS/JHU: Black rubber roofing material 0833UUURBR (Beckman-Nicolet 0.3-14μm)

materials.name: paint_white
materials.source: USGS splib07a: White PVC plastic GDS338 (ASD) — white paint proxy

materials.name: paint_red
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Red nylon ripstop fabric GDS431 (ASD) — red pigment proxy

materials.name: paint_blue
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Blue nylon ripstop fabric GDS433 (ASD) — blue pigment proxy

materials.name: paint_green
materials.source: USGS splib07a: Green fiberglass roofing GDS336 (ASD) — green paint proxy

materials.name: skin_light
materials.source: Digitised from Bashkatov et al. 2005, J. Phys. D 38:2543, Fig. 3
materials.notes: Fair skin in-vivo forearm; 400-2000nm measured, extrapolated outside

materials.name: skin_dark
materials.source: Bashkatov et al. 2005 with increased melanin absorption (visible scaled x0.45)
materials.notes: Dark skin approximation: higher melanin reduces visible reflectance by ~55%

materials.name: hair_dark
materials.source: Bashkatov et al. 2002, Proc. SPIE 4623: dark hair diffuse reflectance ~3-8%
materials.notes: Dark brown/black hair; melanin gives low, slowly rising spectral reflectance
